name: Stuart Leon Bicycle Crash Law
description_short: Philadelphia plaintiff-side law firm exclusively representing bicycle crash victims in PA and NJ. Contingency fee — clients pay nothing unless the firm recovers compensation.
description_long:
Stuart Leon Bicycle Crash Law is a Philadelphia personal injury firm founded in 1982 by Stuart Leon, operating as Stuart Leon, P.C. The firm is headquartered at 1420 Walnut Street, Suite 404, Philadelphia, PA 19102 and is licensed to practice in both Pennsylvania and New Jersey. Stuart Leon holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell, the highest peer-review designation for legal ability and ethical standards, and is a League Certified Instructor (LCI) certified by the League of American Bicyclists — a credential typically held by cycling safety educators, not attorneys. The firm has no financial certifications (CDFI, HUD, NFCC) because it is not a financial services company.

The firm handles exclusively bicycle crash cases — a deliberate and narrow specialty. Services include representation for car-versus-cyclist collisions (covering medical bills, bike damage, and pain and suffering), hit-and-run incidents including Pennsylvania hit-and-run fund claims, road hazard cases involving potholes or defective SEPTA trolley tracks, road rage incidents, and government liability cases where poorly maintained public infrastructure caused the crash. The firm also engages in cyclists' rights advocacy. Clients pay nothing upfront; the firm works on a contingency fee basis, meaning fees are only collected if and when a recovery is made. The exact contingency percentage is not publicly disclosed.

What distinguishes Stuart Leon is the singular focus on cyclists. Unlike general personal injury firms that take car accidents, slip-and-falls, and workplace injuries, this firm turns away cases that don't involve cyclists. Stuart Leon is himself an avid cyclist, and the firm operates as a father-son team with over 40 combined years of experience in this niche. The firm is reportedly in court most weeks actively litigating cases — not just settling. The Philadelphia Citizen profiled Leon as "The Bike Warrior," reflecting a reputation built over four decades in a very specific corner of plaintiff-side litigation.

The firm's core strength is depth over breadth: if you are a cyclist injured in Pennsylvania or New Jersey, few firms can match their institutional knowledge of cycling infrastructure, traffic law as it applies to cyclists, and the specific liability theories involved in road hazard and government entity cases. The primary limitations are geographic (PA and NJ only), scope (strictly bicycle-related crashes — no car accidents unless a cyclist is the victim), and transparency (contingency percentage undisclosed, BBB rating unverified, no online client portal). This is a law firm, not a financial service provider, and its placement in a consumer finance directory reflects a categorical mismatch — consumers should seek it out specifically for bicycle crash legal help, not financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contingency fee structure — no legal fees are owed unless the firm recovers compensation on your behalf. This is standard for plaintiff-side personal injury law, not a guarantee product.

certifications: League Certified Instructor (LCI) — League of American Bicyclists
Pennsylvania Bar (PA Disciplinary Board — no pending proceedings)

pros: Founded in 1982 — over 40 years of experience focused exclusively on bicycle crash cases
AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell, the highest peer-review designation for legal ability and ethical standards
Contingency fee structure means clients pay zero upfront and owe nothing if no recovery is made
Stuart Leon is a League Certified Instructor (LCI), giving the firm genuine technical knowledge of cycling that most attorneys lack
Father-son team actively litigating bicycle cases in court most weeks — not a settlement mill
Handles government liability and road hazard cases (SEPTA tracks, potholes), which require specialized expertise many PI firms avoid
Featured by The Philadelphia Citizen as 'The Bike Warrior' — recognized community figure in Philadelphia cycling advocacy
cons: Geographic scope limited to Pennsylvania and New Jersey — cyclists injured in other states cannot be represented
Strictly handles bicycle-related crashes only — general car accident victims, pedestrians, or other injury types will be turned away
Contingency fee percentage is not publicly disclosed, so clients must inquire directly before signing a retainer
BBB rating is unverified and the firm does not appear in BBB listings as an accredited business
No online client portal or mobile app — communication appears to be traditional phone and in-person
best_for: Cyclists in Pennsylvania or New Jersey who were injured in a crash caused by a motor vehicle driver
Cyclists hit by a driver who fled the scene and need help navigating Pennsylvania's hit-and-run fund
Cyclists injured due to road hazards like potholes, SEPTA trolley tracks, or defective government-maintained infrastructure
Seriously injured cyclists seeking trial-ready representation rather than a quick settlement
diagnosis: Stuart Leon Bicycle Crash Law is the right choice for cyclists in Pennsylvania or New Jersey who have been seriously injured and want representation from attorneys who specialize exclusively in bicycle law — not a generalist firm that occasionally handles bike cases. The main caveat is that this is a plaintiff-side personal injury law firm, not a financial service: it has no place in a credit repair or consumer finance directory, and consumers should understand they are engaging an attorney, not a financial product or counseling service.
services: Car-versus-cyclist crash representation (medical bills, pain and suffering, bike damage)
Road hazard litigation (potholes, defective road surfaces)
SEPTA and trolley track bicycle crash cases
Defective road design claims against municipalities
Government entity liability cases (poorly maintained public infrastructure)
Property damage recovery for bicycle and equipment
